pick.rationale: Alabama holds the edge in this tightly contested Round of 32 matchup primarily due to Texas Tech's devastating loss of star forward JT Toppin to a season-ending ACL tear. While the efficiency metrics are nearly identical, the Crimson Tide's elite #3 ranked offense should exploit Texas Tech's now-diminished offensive capabilities without their Big 12 Player of the Year. Texas Tech's superior defense keeps this competitive, but Alabama's healthy roster and offensive firepower give them the narrow advantage in what should be a close 4 vs 5 seed battle.

reasoningStep.title: Texas Tech vs Alabama Round of 32 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Alabama edges Texas Tech due to JT Toppin's season-ending injury, despite nearly identical efficiency metrics and Texas Tech's defensive advantage.
reasoningStep.evidence: Alabama ranks 16th-17th nationally with elite #3 offense (129 adj_o) but mediocre 67th-ranked defense. Texas Tech ranks 12th-19th with solid 27th-ranked defense but lost star JT Toppin (last year's Big 12 Player of the Year) to torn ACL, severely diminishing their offensive ceiling. Adjusted efficiency margins are nearly equal (Alabama 25.64, Texas Tech 25.19), but the injury disparity tips the balance. Texas Tech still features elite PG Christian Anderson and won their Round of 64 vs Akron, but Alabama's healthy roster and offensive firepower provide the edge in this 4 vs 5 seed matchup.
